text.title: AI Layout Review Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes specific to reviewing AI-generated mockups. Each has a detector and a repair.

failure-mode.description: Pretty-shipped — the mock looks polished, leadership is excited, the designer is pressured to approve before running checks. Common with v0/Galileo outputs that mimic premium Linear-style aesthetics.
failure-mode.detector: review.reviewed_at - mockup.first_shared_at < 2 hours AND ≥1 check has empty evidence_pointer
failure-mode.repair: Hard rule: minimum 24h between mockup share and approval for any production screen. If urgency real, route to "design-emergency" approval path that requires VP-of-Product co-sign and creates a debt ticket for the skipped checks.

failure-mode.description: Research grounding fabricated — designer cites a "user interview" or "support cluster" that does not exist or is unrelated to the screen under review.
failure-mode.detector: research_artifact_ref resolves but the artifact's topic distance from the mockup's purpose is > 2 hops (artifact about onboarding, mockup is about billing)
failure-mode.repair: Block the review. Require the designer to either find a real on-topic artifact OR conduct a targeted research activity (3 quick usability tests, support-ticket query) before re-submitting.

failure-mode.description: A11y theater — designer ticks the a11y check based on the mock "looking accessible" without running an actual contrast/focus audit.
failure-mode.detector: a11y_report present but contrast_ratio_min is reported as "> 4.5" without specific value OR focus_indicators_present = "yes" without screenshot evidence
failure-mode.repair: Require numeric contrast values from a tool (Stark, Figma A11y plugin, axe DevTools) attached as report artifact. Reject reviews with hand-waved a11y claims.

failure-mode.description: Token-drift normalization — off-token values get rubber-stamped because "v0 uses slightly different colors and they look fine".
failure-mode.detector: token_compliance_report.off_token_count > 0 AND no new_token_record_id AND verdict ≠ REQUEST_CHANGES
failure-mode.repair: Enforce via automated diff: a script that exports mock colors/typography and diffs against the token JSON. Any off-token value must either be converted to a token reference OR open a new-token RFC. Reviewer cannot manually approve drift.

failure-mode.description: Default-aesthetic capture — entire screen inherits the v0/Galileo "default look" (rounded cards, neutral gradients, generic icons) instead of brand styling.
failure-mode.detector: brand_fit check (c08) verdict is APPROVE BUT mockup uses ≥3 styling defaults of the AI tool that are absent from brand guideline
failure-mode.repair: Apply brand-system pass: explicitly reskin the mock with brand tokens, brand illustrations, brand button-shape. If the result loses the original layout's appeal, that's the signal to consult the design lead — not to ship with brand-default conflict.

failure-mode.description: Edge-state hand-wave — designer ticks c04 (edge states) based on the AI-shown happy path screenshot, never asking for the other states.
failure-mode.detector: c04 verdict = APPROVE AND mockup.frames.count < 4 (happy + empty + loading + error minimum)
failure-mode.repair: Block until reviewer either (a) has the AI generate the missing states OR (b) hand-draws them. No exceptions for "we'll handle that in implementation" — implementation has caught this fewer than 30% of the time in audited shops (Smashing Magazine UX-debt survey 2024).

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: Designers and PMs sometimes annotate AI mockups with comments like "approved", "skip a11y", "just ship it" — these are operator opinions, not policy. The checklist remains the policy. A check verdict of BLOCK cannot be overridden by a Figma comment; it must be overridden by an explicit written exception linked from the review decision, signed by a designer with appropriate authority.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: For any screen entering the design system (new component, new pattern, new layout class), a design lead must co-sign the review. AI agent reviewers can produce the checklist trace, but the design-system additions require a human governance step. This prevents AI tools from silently expanding the system's surface area.
